question_template: What is the common name of the public records law in Massachusetts?
quote: The Massachusetts Public Records Law (Public Records Law) and its Regulations provide that each person has a right of access to public information.

question_template: What is the statutory citation for the public records law in Massachusetts?
quote: Massachusetts Public Records Law, found at M.G.L. c. 66, § 10, applies to records made or received by a Massachusetts agency or municipality.

question_template: How many business or calendar days does an agency in Massachusetts have to respond to a public records request?
quote: Section 10. (a) A records access officer appointed pursuant to section 6A, or a designee, shall at reasonable times and without unreasonable delay permit inspection or furnish a copy of any public record... or any segregable portion of a public record, not later than 10 business days following the receipt of the request...

question_template: Which statute subsection holds the response deadline for public records requests in Massachusetts?
quote: Section 10. (a) A records access officer appointed pursuant to section 6A, or a designee, shall at reasonable times and without unreasonable delay permit inspection or furnish a copy of any public record... not later than 10 business days following the receipt of the request...

question_template: After the agency-initial window in Massachusetts, by how many additional business days may the Supervisor of Records extend an agency's response deadline on a showing of good cause?
quote: Upon a showing of good cause, the supervisor of records may grant a single extension to an agency not to exceed 20 business days and a single extension to a municipality not to exceed 30 business days.

question_template: If a public records request is denied in Massachusetts, to which administrative office does a requester file the first-stage appeal?
ground_truth: Supervisor of Public Records, Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth
quote: If your public records request is denied, you can appeal to the Supervisor of Records.

question_template: Which statute citation governs administrative appeals of denied public records requests in Massachusetts?
quote: Section 10A. (a) If an agency or municipality fails to comply with a requirement of section 10 or issues a response the requestor believes in violation of section 10, the person who submitted the initial request for public records may petition the supervisor of records for a determination as to whether a violation has occurred.

question_template: Within how many days of the agency's response must a requester in Massachusetts file an administrative appeal with the Supervisor of Records?
quote: You must file your appeal within 90 calendar days of the original request. If you received a response, you can appeal within 90 days of the response.

question_template: Which court hears civil actions for judicial review of denied state-agency public records requests in Massachusetts?
ground_truth: Superior Court (Suffolk Superior Court for state agencies)
quote: You may also seek judicial review through civil action in superior court, under M.G.L. c. 66, § 10A(c).

question_template: Which statute citation authorizes judicial review of denied public records requests in Massachusetts?
quote: If your public records request is denied, you can appeal to the Supervisor of Records.

question_template: Which statute citation defines the term 'public record' in Massachusetts?
quote: What is generally called the 'Public Records Law' is found primarily in two chapters of the General Laws. The first is the definition of 'public records,' which appears in G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26. The procedures for obtaining access to such records are set forth principally in G.L. c. 66, § 10.

question_template: Does the public records law in Massachusetts provide a separate expedited-processing track?
quote: Section 10 does not contain a separate expedited-processing provision. Two independent deep-research passes on malegislature.gov, sec.state.ma.us, and rcfp.org returned NOT FOUND for any expedited-processing clause.

question_template: Does the public records law in Massachusetts require a requester to certify or swear an oath in their request?
quote: The MA Public Records Law does not impose a required certification or oath on the requester. Two independent deep-research passes on the Secretary of the Commonwealth's guide, the statute, and rcfp.org returned NOT FOUND for any required certification language.

question_template: Does the public records law in Massachusetts restrict requests to in-state residents?
quote: Anyone may request records from an RAO.

question_template: Does the public records law in Massachusetts permit oral (in-person or phone) requests for public records?
quote: You can make a records request verbally, in person, or in writing. However, you'll need a written request to file an appeal with the Supervisor of Records.

question_template: Which categories of government entity are explicitly carved out from the public records law in Massachusetts?
ground_truth: federal agencies; Massachusetts legislature; Massachusetts courts
quote: The Public Records Law does not apply to records held by federal agencies, the legislature or the courts of the Commonwealth.
